excerpt: Hue was described as a city of about 140,000 people.
confidence_note: Strong baseline city-population anchor for the civilian denominator.

excerpt: By battle's end, about 115,000 people were temporarily homeless.
confidence_note: Useful for displacement intensity, but homelessness is not the same as exit from the exposed area.

excerpt: One official history identified 5,000 refugees in a Catholic church, 17,000 at Hue University, and another 40,000 displaced in the Citadel sector.
transform_note: Summed the three explicitly reported refuge/displacement counts: 5,000 + 17,000 + 40,000 = 62,000.
confidence_note: Partial snapshot of internal displacement, not a full city-wide outflow count.

excerpt: The history reports 5,800 civilians 'dead or missing' out of a population of about 140,000.
transform_note: Excluded from core civilian direct-death numerator because it combines deaths and missing persons without a clean direct/indirect or status split.
confidence_note: Important context, but not admissible as the baseline direct-death count.

excerpt: Villard breaks allied deaths into Marines 142, ARVN 333, Vietnamese Marines 88, 1st Cavalry 68, and 101st Airborne 6.
confidence_note: Best official-history all-allied KIA aggregation for the whole Hue episode.

excerpt: Reported wounded totals are 1,100 Marines, 1,773 ARVN, 350 Vietnamese Marines, 453 1st Cavalry, and 56 101st Airborne.
confidence_note: Useful allied injury series, though not a full all-combatant injury count.

excerpt: Official allied estimate for Communist dead ranges from 2,500 to 5,000.
confidence_note: Core uncertainty driver: this is an estimate band, not a named-victim count.

excerpt: A captured-document floor reports 1,042 enemy killed.
confidence_note: Likely a floor or partial accounting, but it is the most conservative combatant-death figure explicitly tied to captured records.

excerpt: Camp reports that 15-18 enemy battalions took part in the battle.
confidence_note: Useful for force-exposure modeling, especially on the enemy side.

excerpt: Camp estimates 8,000-11,000 enemy troops in the city or direct approaches.
confidence_note: Primary enemy force-size anchor for military person-month scenarios.

geographic_scope: Hue city and Phu Bai/Camp Evans vicinity
excerpt: Hanger notes less than 1,000 South Vietnamese defenders, a few hundred U.S. personnel in the city, Task Force X-Ray's three Marine battalions, and nearby 1st Cavalry/101st brigades.
transform_note: Used qualitatively to bound opening-phase military exposure; no single force total is stated for all directly assigned units.
confidence_note: Important context for why a simple midpoint military denominator would be misleading.

excerpt: Hanger summarizes Operation Hue City as four phases, which is used to phase-weight force and civilian exposure.
confidence_note: Supports the episodeization and person-month reconstruction.

geographic_scope: northwest, south, southwest, and eastern approaches to Hue
excerpt: On 17 February, Hanger notes the arrival of the 3d Brigade/1st Cavalry, the 1st Brigade/101st Airborne, and two Marine battalions to seal the city.
transform_note: Used to raise late-phase military exposure above the opening-phase baseline.
confidence_note: Key support for the modeled increase in military person-months after 17 February.

excerpt: Hanger identifies three 1st Cavalry battalions severing enemy resupply lines west of the city on 21-23 February.
confidence_note: Helps justify including immediate western blocking forces in the military denominator.

excerpt: Villard explicitly reports more than 4,000 confirmed civilian deaths during the 25-day battle.
transform_note: Used as the baseline floor for the core civilian direct-death estimate; later estimate high is kept only slightly above this because the cleaner figure above 4,000 is not specified.
confidence_note: Best available explicit direct civilian death count for the core DR path.

excerpt: Of the confirmed civilian fatalities, about 1,200 were attributed to bombs and bullets.
confidence_note: Important because it confirms that crossfire/air/artillery deaths are part of the core direct-death civilian numerator.

geographic_scope: Hue and immediate associated grave sites
excerpt: Villard reports approximately 3,000 civilians in mass graves and at least 600 buried alive.
transform_note: This component is treated cautiously because later exhumation/attribution debates affect whether all grave victims were civilians executed by Communist cadres rather than killed in battle.
confidence_note: Useful as a component claim, but also the main source of disagreement in the civilian-death discussion.

geographic_scope: Hue and surrounding grave sites connected to the battle
excerpt: Willbanks summarizes later discoveries as 2,800 bodies unearthed from mass graves.
transform_note: Stored as context rather than folded directly into a separate core estimate because grave sites extended beyond the immediate city and status attribution is disputed.
confidence_note: Important corroboration that later body recoveries were large, but not a cleaner replacement for the Villard >4,000 figure.

excerpt: Willbanks gives a lower allied KIA aggregation of 384 ARVN, 147 Marines, and 74 Army dead.
transform_note: Summed the explicit component KIA: 384 + 147 + 74 = 605. This source does not list Vietnamese Marine deaths separately, so it is treated as a conservative low-bound alternative rather than the preferred best estimate.
confidence_note: Useful low-end allied KIA check.

excerpt: Willbanks reports 1,800 ARVN wounded, 857 Marine wounded, and 507 Army wounded.
transform_note: Summed explicit wounded counts: 1,800 + 857 + 507 = 3,164. Treated as a conservative low-end injury series because Vietnamese Marine wounded are not separately stated here.
confidence_note: Useful lower-bound allied injury check.

excerpt: Porter cites physician Alje Vennema's much smaller count of 68 bodies at four major sites versus an official 477.
transform_note: This is not used as a substitute civilian-death numerator; it is retained to document why the grave evidence is contested.
confidence_note: Important disagreement source; status and attribution remain unresolved.

excerpt: Porter argues that most 1969-recovered bodies were battle victims rather than execution victims.
transform_note: Used only to widen attribution uncertainty and to discourage overconfident massacre-only classification.
confidence_note: Interpretive counter-source; does not generate a cleaner all-civilian direct-death total.
